---
title: "About This Website"
---

This portfolio presents a collection of ten data analysis projects focused on extracting and interpreting information from open-source repositories. The primary objective is to move beyond pre-curated datasets and develop a complete workflow for data acquisition and analysis. Using web scraping techniques - particularly with Wikipedia’s historical and geographical archives - unstructured HTML is converted into structured datasets suitable for analysis. Each R Markdown file serves as an independent investigation into a specific topic.

The technical foundation of these projects lies in the integration of data extraction and visualization. The R programming language is used to scrape, clean, and organize data, with attention to consistent formatting and accurate variable representation. Visualizations are used to support interpretation by making patterns and relationships easier to identify, including both geospatial and distributional approaches.

Each file follows a consistent workflow: an initial research question, followed by data collection and cleaning, and concluding with analysis and interpretation. Many of the topics involve incomplete or inconsistently formatted records, requiring a structured approach to transform descriptive listings into usable datasets. The overall aim is to produce clear, reproducible analyses that highlight patterns related to time, location, and human activity.
